mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase corporate borrowing costs and raise equity discount rates, directly suppressing S&P 500 valuations and generating negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no significant negative correlation with rate increases.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.2 percent, labor market slack accelerates rapidly, signaling imminent corporate earnings deterioration and triggering negative equity returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 65 percent of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when unemployment stays below 4.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales combined with stable consumer price inflation indicates robust consumer demand without monetary tightening pressure, driving positive forward equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-series baseline, or 60-day forward returns turn negative when retail sales growth exceeds 2 percent annually.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, industrial production growth outpaces policy rate hikes, confirming economic resilience and generating positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 55 percent of tightening periods, or industrial production declines coincide with positive equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: Increasing housing starts reflect strong construction sector demand and rising household wealth effects, producing positive forward equity returns across market cycles.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.01 over a 5-year sample, or housing starts growth consistently precedes negative S&P 500 returns.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index exceeds 320, persistent inflation forces aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and generating negative forward returns.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive in over 70 percent of high-inflation observations, or the threshold loses predictive significance after 2020.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous payroll employment growth and declining unemployment rates confirm a strengthening labor market, boosting corporate revenue expectations and driving positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample tests, or 20-day returns are negative when both labor indicators improve simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansions, slowing GDP growth alongside elevated policy rates signals a growth recession, causing negative forward equity returns as earnings forecasts are downgraded.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 60 percent of late-cycle observations, or the GDP-rate interaction fails to predict return direction.
